value: beads documentation follows 4-layer ownership: (1) bd prime is authoritative for live workflow commands and session protocols, (2) ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md has only machine-wide invariants (beads mandatory, no TodoWrite, use bd remember not MEMORY.md), (3) AGENTS.md has bd onboard snippet + repo-specific deltas (hardware testing, multi-agent coordination, recording decision tree), (4) CLAUDE.md has technical context only with pointer to AGENTS.md. Multi-agent primitives available in bd 0.58.0: swarm, slot, agent state, gate, merge-slot, audit.

The all_hardware feature lives on bin and driver-registry crates, NOT on server. CI feature-matrix.yml must use package: bin for all_hardware mock testing. Ring buffer benchmark runs twice in CI (workspace tests + dedicated step); the dedicated step uses continue-on-error:true since it's purely for metrics reporting.

Deploy scripts: deploy-maitai.sh (includes cargo clean), deploy-leabs.sh (NO cargo clean, must source cargo env for SSH). Both support --daemon-only, --gui-only, --skip-build, --branch flags. WASM build: trunk build --release in crates/ui, serve dist/ with python3 -m http.server 8080

Hardware-in-the-loop development cycle: 1) bd ready (find work) → 2) code changes → 3) deploy daemon (deploy-*.sh --daemon-only) → 4) navigate Chrome to WASM GUI → 5) connect to daemon, verify devices → 6) bd create --type=bug for issues found → 7) fix and re-test → 8) bd close, git push

Agent worktrees at .claude/worktrees/agent-*. Shared git objects — branches visible across worktrees. Clean up with git worktree remove <path> --force. Git config lock can occur during parallel creation — retry if needed. Use bd-safe.sh for worktree-safe beads commands.

Leabs-dev daemon at http://10.0.0.40:50051 (no Tailscale, local network only) has 3 devices: istar_camera (Andor iStar sCMOS), ipg_laser (IPG YLPP-200-1-50-R), power_meter (Thorlabs PM400). Use --hardware-config config/leabs_hardware.toml (mutually exclusive with --runtime-mode)

Maitai daemon at http://100.117.5.12:50051 has 12 devices: prime_bsi (PVCAM), photodiode+ni_daq_ao0+ni_daq_ao1 (Comedi), rotator_2/3/8 (ELL14), maitai (Ti:Sapph laser), power_meter (Newport 1830-C), esp300_axis1/2/3 (only axis1 connected)

driver-registry crate owns register_all_factories and create_registry_from_config. common crate owns DriverFactory trait (driver.rs:668), capability traits, Parameter<T>, DaqError. hardware crate owns DeviceRegistry (DashMap). All driver crates depend on common, NOT hardware (dependency inversion). plugin-api and plugin-example crates DELETED.

1340 cast warnings after changing lints to warn. Top hotspots: ui/image_viewer (80+77), roi_selector (61), driver-mock/pattern (58). 653 unsafe blocks total, 92% in FFI driver crates (justified). 51 blocks missing SAFETY comments (41 Comedi, 10 Andor).

cargo nextest run --workspace --profile ci runs ~2519 tests in ~35s. 2 known flaky tests: test_golden_startup_runtime_mode_universal, test_golden_mock_devices_registered. Pre-commit hooks: fmt, ast-grep, trailing whitespace, merge conflicts, private key detection.
WASM GUI served on maitai at http://100.117.5.12:8080 — Claude Code can interact with real hardware through Chrome browser automation (claude-in-chrome MCP)
